title: Adana Kebab

seo.title: Authentic Adana Kebab Recipe | Make it at Home!
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Adana Kebab at home with our easy-to-follow recipe. Get juicy, flavorful Turkish skewers perfect for lunch or dinner. Try it today!
seo.h1: Authentic Adana Kebab Recipe: Make Delicious Turkish Skewers at Home
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Adana Kebab
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Adana Kebab
Pro Tips for the Best Adana Kebab Results
seo.imageAlt: Platter of freshly grilled authentic Adana Kebab skewers with grilled vegetables and flatbread
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy the rich, spicy flavors of homemade Adana Kebab, a true Turkish delight perfect for any meal.

description: Adana Kebab is a renowned Turkish specialty featuring hand-minced lamb or beef, expertly seasoned with red pepper flakes and sumac, then grilled on wide skewers. This iconic dish from Adana is famous for its rich, spicy flavor and tender texture, often served with fresh vegetables and lavash. It's a delightful and robust meal perfect for any time of day.

ingredients.notes: finely diced, optional for extra juiciness

instructions.title: Prepare the Meat Mixture
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the ground lamb, finely chopped red and green bell peppers, red onion, and minced garlic. Ensure all vegetables are very finely diced to integrate well into the meat.

instructions.title: Season the Kebab
instructions.text: Add red pepper flakes, sweet paprika, ground cumin, sumac, salt, and black pepper to the meat mixture. If using, add the finely diced lamb fat (kuyruk yagi) for an authentic flavor and juiciness.

instructions.title: Knead Thoroughly
instructions.text: With clean hands, knead the mixture vigorously for at least 10-15 minutes until it becomes very cohesive and sticky. This critical step develops the meat's proteins, preventing it from falling off the skewers.

instructions.title: Chill the Mixture
instructions.text: Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate the meat mixture for at least 1 hour, or preferably 2-3 hours. This chilling process helps the flavors meld and the meat firm up for easier shaping onto the skewers.

instructions.title: Shape the Kebabs
instructions.text: Moisten your hands with a little water. Take a portion of the chilled meat mixture (about 150-200g) and press it firmly around a wide, flat metal skewer. Shape it evenly along the skewer, ensuring it is compact and secure. Repeat for all skewers.

instructions.title: Grill the Adana Kebabs
instructions.text: Preheat your grill to medium-high heat. Place the prepared skewers on the hot grill. Grill for about 3-5 minutes per side, turning frequently, until cooked through, nicely charred, and juicy.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Remove the kebabs from the grill. Carefully slide them off the skewers onto a serving platter. Serve hot with grilled tomatoes, peppers, sliced onions with sumac and parsley, and fresh lavash bread or pita.

tips: For authentic Adana Kebab, use lamb with a good fat content (around 20-25%). The fat renders during grilling, keeping the kebab moist and flavorful. If your lamb is too lean, add a little extra lamb tail fat (kuyruk yagi) or a tablespoon of olive oil.

The kneading process is vital. It develops the protein strands, making the meat mixture sticky and preventing it from falling off the skewers. Don't skimp on this step; it's the secret to a perfect Adana Kebab.

Serve Adana Kebab with a simple shepherd's salad (çoban salata), grilled vegetables (tomatoes, green peppers), and a generous sprinkle of sumac on thinly sliced red onions for a complete and traditional Turkish experience.
article: Adana Kebab is a beloved Turkish Lunch Recipes dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
